message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  The recent pandemic of coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19) is caused by severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2). COVID-19 was first reported in China (December 2019) and now prevalent in ∼170 countries across the globe. Entry of SARS-CoV-2 into mammalian cells require the binding of viral Spike (S) proteins to the ACE2 (angiotensin converting enzyme 2) receptor. Once entered the S protein is primed by a specialised serine protease, TMPRSS2 (Transmembrane Serine Protease 2) in the host cell. Importantly, beside respiratory symptoms, consistent with other common respiratory virus infection when patients become viraemic, a significant number of COVID-19 patients also develop liver comorbidities. We explored if specific target cell-type in the mammalian liver, could be implicated in disease pathophysiology other than the general deleterious response to cytokine storms. Here we employed single-cell RNA-seq (scRNA-seq) to survey the human liver and identified potentially implicated liver cell-type for viral ingress. We report the co-expression of ACE2 and TMPRSS2 in a TROP2
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  liver progenitor population. Importantly, we fail to detect the expression of ACE2 in hepatocyte or any other liver (immune and stromal) cell types. These results indicated that in COVID-19 associated liver dysfunction and cell death, viral infection of TROP2
                  <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                  progenitors in liver may significantly impaired liver regeneration and could lead to pathology.
                </jats:p>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Highlights</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="simple">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        - EPCAM
                        <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                        Liver progenitors co-express ACE2 and TMPRSS2
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        - ACE2 and TMPRSS2 expression is highest in TROP2
                        <jats:sup>high</jats:sup>
                        progenitors
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>- ACE2 and TMPRSS2 cells express cholangiocyte biased fate markers</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>- ACE2 and TMPRSS2 positive cells are absent in human fetal liver</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title:
scRNA-seq reveals ACE2 and TMPRSS2 expression in TROP2
                  <sup>+</sup>
                  Liver Progenitor Cells: Implications in COVID-19 associated Liver Dysfunction
